description: Visible scratches along the length of developed film, typically on the base (shiny) side.
steps: Remove cartridge and open cartridge door
Inspect pressure plate under 10x loupe for burrs, scratches, or debris
Inspect film channel guide surfaces for rough spots or embedded particles
If pressure plate shows wear marks deeper than 0.01 mm, replace it (see S8C-102 procedure)
If film channel shows scratches, unclip and replace (see S8C-105 procedure)
Clean all film-contact surfaces with lens tissue and isopropyl alcohol
Run a test strip through the gate to verify smooth transport

description: Frames on developed film are not evenly spaced — some overlap or have gaps between them.
steps: Remove film gate and pressure plate to access claw
Inspect claw tip under magnification — look for wear, rounding, or chipping
Check claw pivot pin for play (should be zero)
Manually rotate the shaft and watch claw engagement — it should enter perforations cleanly
If claw tip shows visible wear, replace the claw mechanism (see S8C-103 procedure)
If engagement is sloppy despite good claw, check cam follower (see S8C-104 procedure)
After replacement, verify 4.234 mm pulldown stroke with dial indicator

symptom: Motor runs but film doesn't advance
description: Camera motor spins audibly but film stays stationary in the cartridge.
steps: Open gearbox housing (remove 4x cover screws)
Inspect all 4 gears for stripped, chipped, or worn teeth
Check that motor pinion meshes with stage 1 gear — look for a gap
Check stage 2 output gear engagement with main shaft
If any gear teeth are damaged, replace the complete gear set
Apply fresh Superlube grease to all gear teeth
Reassemble and run motor for 10 seconds to break in new gears

description: Pressing the trigger produces no motor response — camera appears completely dead.
partsNeeded: S8C-501 PCB ($25) if board is damaged
steps: Check batteries — measure voltage with multimeter (should be > 5.0V total)
Replace batteries if voltage is low
Check battery contacts for corrosion — clean with pencil eraser if needed
Verify J3 power connector is fully seated on PCB
Check trigger switch — press trigger and listen for microswitch click
Verify J4 connector (trigger signal) is seated on PCB
If all connections are good and batteries are fresh, the PCB may need replacement

description: All frames appear soft or out of focus, even with a sharp lens and proper focus setting.
partsNeeded: S8C-701 Lens Mount ($35) if flange distance is wrong
steps: Remove lens and inspect rear element for dust, fingerprints, or damage
Clean lens with lens tissue and blower
Check lens mount flange distance with depth gauge — must be 17.526 mm +/- 0.02 mm
If flange distance is off, the mount may have shifted — remove and reseat it
Inspect film gate aperture for debris that could scatter light
Check pressure plate — if film isn't flat against the gate, images will be soft
If mount threads are damaged, replace the lens mount (see S8C-701 procedure)

description: Developed film shows fogging, streaks, or bright spots from stray light entering the camera body.
steps: In a dark room, place a flashlight inside the camera body
Inspect all seams, door edges, and joints for visible light escaping
Check cartridge door foam seal — replace if compressed, torn, or missing
Check battery door seal condition
Inspect body halves junction — re-torque M2.5 screws if loose
If cartridge door seal groove is damaged, replace the door
Re-test with flashlight after repairs

description: Projected film shows visible speed changes or exposure flicker between frames.
steps: Run camera with no cartridge and listen for irregular motor sound
Check gear teeth for wear or debris (open gearbox housing)
Spin main shaft by hand — it should rotate with uniform resistance
If shaft feels gritty or has dead spots, replace bearings
If gears show wear, replace gear set and lubricate
Check encoder flag on shutter disc — should be clean and undamaged
If motor itself is failing, replace the motor (S8C-301)

description: The trigger feels soft, doesn't click, or requires excessive force. Camera may start intermittently.
steps: Remove bottom plate to access trigger mechanism from below
Check trigger return spring — should push lever back firmly
Listen for microswitch click when pressing trigger
Check J4 connector pin 3 (TRIG_SW) for continuity when pressed
If spring is weak or broken, or microswitch doesn't click, replace trigger assembly
Reinstall and verify clean, crisp trigger action
